body: {"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. A harness child spawned by the execute-bead executor does NOT survive abnormal death of its parent worker (fake harness binary forks grandchild, SIGKILL parent, assert child+grandchild gone within bounded grace; Linux via SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig).","coverage":"Child 1 (Pdeathsig) AC1 TestExecutor_OrphanKilledOnParentSIGKILL"},{"parent_ac":"2. cmdSetProcessGroup sets Pdeathsig (Linux) and the thread-migration caveat is handled (LockOSThread or equivalent); a test or documented rationale covers the non-Linux fallback.","coverage":"Child 1 (Pdeathsig) AC2 TestCmdSetProcessGroup_Pdeathsig and AC3 (thread-migration guard + non-Linux fallback documented)"},{"parent_ac":"3. `ddx work` startup reaps orphaned execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace, and does NOT kill harness children of other live workers (plant orphan + live-owned, assert only orphan reaped).","coverage":"Child 2 (startup reaper) AC1 TestWork_ReapsOrphanedHarnessChildren and AC2 TestWork_DoesNotReapLiveOwnedChildren"},{"parent_ac":"4. The docker-clone backend (attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit; covered by a test or, if teardown already guarantees this, a documented justification.","coverage":"Child 3 (docker-clone cleanup) AC1 TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup and AC2 (documented teardown justification)"},{"parent_ac":"5. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown still terminates in-flight harness children (regression guard).","coverage":"Child 1 (Pdeathsig) AC4 TestExecutor_GracefulShutdownKillsChild"},{"parent_ac":"6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"Child 1 AC5/AC6, Child 2 AC4/AC5, Child 3 AC3/AC4 (each child carries the go test ./internal/agent/... [+ ./cmd/... for the reaper] and lefthook run pre-commit gates)"}],"child_ids":["ddx-b06b7e85","ddx-c68f3f22","ddx-3113d524"],"rationale":"The parent bundles three independently shippable fixes plus one regression guard, each touching a distinct file/seam. Child 1 is the primary kernel-enforced fix (Pdeathsig in executor_unix.go:13-15, with the thread-migration caveat and non-Linux fallback) and owns the graceful-shutdown regression guard since it is the change most likely to regress that path. Child 2 is the cross-platform backstop reaper at `ddx work` startup (root.go:49-50 gap), deliberately independent of Pdeathsig so it covers reboots, pre-Pdeathsig binaries, and misfires; it carries the ddx-8f2e0ebf lease-release tie-in. Child 3 closes the docker-clone backend hole (attempt_backend.go:293) that bypasses the executor's Setpgid path entirely. All parent non-scope items (no regression of graceful shutdown, fizeau-50b6f055 boundary), the cross-refs (fizeau-50b6f055, ddx-8f2e0ebf), and the go test + lefthook gates are preserved across the children. No blocking dependency IDs existed in the parent (fizeau-50b6f055 and ddx-8f2e0ebf were cross-refs, retained as ref: labels)."}

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. A harness child spawned by the execute-bead executor does NOT survive abnormal death of its parent worker. Verified by a test that starts the executor against a fake harness binary which forks a long-lived grandchild, then SIGKILLs the parent worker process (not graceful SIGTERM) and asserts the harness child + grandchild are gone (not PID-1 orphans) within a bounded grace. On Linux this is satisfied via SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig in cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go.","coverage":"Child 1 AC1 covers abnormal SIGKILL of the parent worker with a fake harness and long-lived grandchild; Child 1 AC2 covers Linux `SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig` in `cmdSetProcessGroup`."},{"parent_ac":"2. cmdSetProcessGroup sets Pdeathsig (Linux) and the thread-migration caveat is handled (LockOSThread or equivalent) so the death signal actually fires; a test or documented rationale covers the non-Linux fallback.","coverage":"Child 1 AC2 covers `cmdSetProcessGroup` setting `Pdeathsig` and `Setpgid`; Child 1 AC3 covers the thread-migration caveat; Child 1 AC5 covers the non-Linux fallback."},{"parent_ac":"3. `ddx work` startup reaps orphaned execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace, and does NOT kill harness children of other live workers. Verified by a test that plants a fake orphaned harness process (parent gone) and a fake live-owned one and asserts only the orphan is reaped.","coverage":"Child 2 AC1 covers reaping project-scoped orphaned execute-bead harness processes; Child 2 AC2 covers not killing live-owned harnesses; Child 2 AC3 covers not killing other-workspace harnesses; Child 2 AC4 covers operator-attention evidence."},{"parent_ac":"4. The docker-clone backend (attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit; covered by a test or, if container teardown already guarantees this, a documented justification.","coverage":"Child 3 AC1 covers process-group setup for the docker invocation at `attempt_backend.go:293`; Child 3 AC2 covers container teardown on worker cancellation; Child 3 AC3 covers abnormal parent-death cleanup or documented justification."},{"parent_ac":"5. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown still terminates in-flight harness children (regression guard).","coverage":"Child 1 AC4 covers graceful signal/context cleanup for executor harness trees; Child 2 AC5 preserves the same regression guard while adding startup reaping; Child 3 AC4 preserves the same regression guard for docker-clone changes."},{"parent_ac":"6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"Child 1 AC6 and AC7 require `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit`; Child 2 AC6 and AC7 require the same gates; Child 3 AC5 and AC6 require the same gates."}],"child_ids":["ddx-0d7d5f0e","ddx-7e3e7b63","ddx-e582c37a"],"rationale":"The parent bead contains three separable failure surfaces: Linux executor parent-death enforcement, startup cleanup of already-orphaned or non-Linux harnesses, and docker-clone backend cleanup. Splitting along those boundaries keeps each child independently executable with focused tests while preserving the shared non-scope constraints, cross-references to fizeau-50b6f055 and ddx-8f2e0ebf, and the required `go test` plus `lefthook` verification gates."}

Verified by a test that starts the executor against a fake harness binary which forks a long-lived grandchild, then SIGKILLs the parent worker process (not graceful SIGTERM) and asserts the harness child + grandchild are gone (not PID-1 orphans) within a bounded grace. On Linux this is satisfied via SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig in cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go.","coverage":"executor parent-death child AC 1 covers the direct harness child on SIGKILL with Linux Pdeathsig; startup reaper child AC 1 and AC 2 cover orphaned harness plus grandchild process groups within bounded grace."},{"parent_ac":"2. cmdSetProcessGroup sets Pdeathsig (Linux) and the thread-migration caveat is handled (LockOSThread or equivalent) so the death signal actually fires; a test or documented rationale covers the non-Linux fallback.","coverage":"executor parent-death child AC 2 covers Pdeathsig plus Setpgid; AC 3 covers the LockOSThread or equivalent thread-migration guard; AC 4 covers the non-Linux fallback/rationale."},{"parent_ac":"3. `ddx work` startup reaps orphaned execute-bead harness children from prior dead workers scoped to the current project/workspace, and does NOT kill harness children of other live workers. Verified by a test that plants a fake orphaned harness process (parent gone) and a fake live-owned one and asserts only the orphan is reaped.","coverage":"startup reaper child AC 1 covers current-workspace orphan reaping; AC 2 covers grandchild process groups; AC 3 covers not killing live-owned harnesses; AC 4 covers not killing other-workspace harnesses; AC 5 covers operator-attention evidence."},{"parent_ac":"4. The docker-clone backend (attempt_backend.go:293) configures process-group/container cleanup so its nested harness is reaped on worker exit; covered by a test or, if container teardown already guarantees this, a documented justification.","coverage":"docker-clone child AC 1 covers process-group cleanup on the docker command; AC 2 covers container/nested harness cleanup on cancellation or worker exit; AC 3 covers documented teardown guarantee if relying on Docker behavior."},{"parent_ac":"5. Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown still terminates in-flight harness children (regression guard).","coverage":"executor parent-death child AC 5 covers graceful cancellation/SIGTERM-style process-group cleanup regression; docker-clone child AC 2 covers graceful worker exit for docker-clone cleanup."},{"parent_ac":"6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes.","coverage":"executor parent-death child AC 7 and AC 8 preserve `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit`; startup reaper child AC 7 and AC 8 preserve package-level go test plus lefthook gates for cmd/internal agent scope; docker-clone child AC 6 and AC 7 preserve internal/agent go test plus lefthook gates."}],"child_ids":["ddx-f39e5d40","ddx-418c3849","ddx-f04a4fe0"],"rationale":"The parent bead combines three independently executable implementation scopes: Linux executor parent-death signaling, workspace-scoped startup orphan reaping, and docker-clone backend cleanup. Splitting along those process-lifetime boundaries keeps each child small enough to implement and test independently while preserving the original non-scope items, fizeau-50b6f055 and ddx-8f2e0ebf cross-references, and all go test/lefthook verification requirements."}

verification_command passed: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...
powerClass=smart
rationale: verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...

All six acceptance criteria for ddx-93d2ac52 are already provably satisfied by
code and tests that landed in prior child beads (ddx-f39e5d40 pdeathsig executor
tests, ddx-f04a4fe0 docker-clone process-group tests). No further code change is
required; adding redundant tests would violate YAGNI/DOWITYTD.

Evidence per AC:

AC1 (harness child does not survive abnormal parent SIGKILL via Pdeathsig):
  - cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:28 sets SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig = SIGKILL.
  - Test TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_LinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL
    (cli/internal/agent/executor_linux_test.go:74) starts the executor against a
    helper that forks a long-lived grandchild, SIGKILLs the parent worker (not a
    graceful SIGTERM), and asserts child+grandchild are gone and not PID-1
    orphans within a bounded grace.

AC2 (Pdeathsig set + thread-migration caveat handled + non-Linux fallback):
  - LockOSThread/UnlockOSThread around cmd.Start() at executor.go:183-185.
  - TestCmdSetProcessGroup_LinuxConfiguresPdeathsigAndSetpgid (executor_linux_test.go:61).
  - TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_ParentDeathSignalThreadGuard (executor_linux_test.go:142).
  - TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_NonLinuxParentDeathFallbackDocumentsGap
    (executor_notlinux_test.go:24) documents and verifies the non-Linux fallback.

AC3 (ddx work startup reaps orphans scoped to project; spares live-owned children):
  - reapOrphanedHarnessChildren wired into startup at execute_bead_loop.go:1246.
  - TestWorkStartup_ReapsOrphanedHarnessChildren,
    TestWorkStartup_DoesNotReapLiveOwnedChildren, and
    TestWorkStartup_ReaperDoesNotKillOtherWorkspaceHarness
    (orphan_harness_reaper_test.go:63,134,206).

AC4 (docker-clone backend process-group/container cleanup):
  - dockerAttemptCommand applies cmdSetProcessGroup (attempt_backend.go:799);
    dockerRunArgs uses --rm --init (attempt_backend.go:727).
  - Tests in attempt_backend_unix_test.go (TestDockerCloneBackend_ProcessGroupCleanup
    and siblings).

AC5 (graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM regression guard):
  - TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_GracefulCancellationStillKillsProcessGroup
    (executor_linux_test.go:157).

AC6 (cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green; lefthook run pre-commit passes):
  - Full agent package suite passes locally (108s); see verification_command above.
